operating_point_stability_sf_corrected.method: SF-corrected 3-tag R_b stability across 15 threshold configurations, 10% data

operating_point_stability_fulldata.method: 3-tag R_b stability across 8 configurations, full data
operating_point_stability_fulldata.note: Poor chi2 driven by WP-dependent systematic not captured by stat-only combination. R_b value is ~12% below SM.

kappa_consistency.method: A_FB^b purity-corrected across kappa (MC expected, Phase 4a)

operating_point_stability_sf_fulldata.method: SF-corrected 3-tag R_b stability across 15 threshold configurations, full data

kappa_consistency_fulldata.method: A_FB^b inclusive (slope/delta_b) across 4 kappa values, full data
kappa_consistency_fulldata.note: Poor chi2 reflects kappa-dependent charm contamination in inclusive method. Higher kappa gives larger A_FB^b (0.0027 at k=2 vs -0.0017 at k=0.3) because delta_b grows faster than delta_c.

afb_sign_investigation.root_cause: estimate_purity_at_wp had calibration only at WPs 9.0/10.0, returning f_b~0.19 and f_c~0.40 for ALL WPs. The charm correction dominated the small slope.
afb_sign_investigation.mc_validation: MC slopes consistent with zero (A_FB^b ~ -0.02 +/- 0.02 at WP 5.0)

R_b_vs_ALEPH.explanation: Simplified single-tag system (vs ALEPH 5-tag), no per-hemisphere vertex reconstruction, eps_c > eps_b tag inversion, WP-dependent Rb.

R_b_vs_LEP_combined.explanation: Simplified single-tag system (vs ALEPH 5-tag), no per-hemisphere vertex reconstruction, eps_c > eps_b tag inversion, WP-dependent Rb.

A_FB_b_vs_ALEPH.note: Our statistical uncertainty is artificially small because the inclusive method neglects charm contamination. The uncertainty is underestimated.

A_FB_b_vs_LEP_combined.note: Large discrepancy from LEP combined. Our inclusive method underestimates A_FB^b because it does not correct for charm/uds contamination in the tagged sample. The b-purity is only ~20%.
